description: An access-master record naming a set of namespaced permissions. A user references one role; the role's permissions are the primary of the four grant sources (role + direct user grant + designation + business-unit) that union into a user's effective permission set. Two roles are seeded: SystemAdmin (granted the super-permission `all`) and User. Roles are defined by the Administrator during configuration.

fields.name: roleId
fields.label: Role No.
fields.purpose: Stable key a user's roleId foreign-keys to so a role can be renamed or re-permissioned without re-keying every user.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Role Name
fields.validation: non-blank; unique; e.g. SystemAdmin, User, Buyer, FinanceMaker; the display field for the role reference
fields.purpose: The role's name shown in the user form's role dropdown; SystemAdmin and User are seeded, and the name is what an admin recognizes when assigning a role to a user.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.validation: max 500 chars; what the role is for
fields.purpose: Documents the role's intent so an admin granting it understands the access it confers; informational for the RBAC configuration screen.

fields.name: permissions
fields.label: Permissions
fields.validation: namespaced permission values (resource.action); SystemAdmin holds the super-permission `all`; the role's contribution to the effective-permission union
fields.purpose: The permissions the role grants; the primary of the four sources deduplicated into a user's effective set. SystemAdmin's single `all` short-circuits every guard; other roles hold an explicit list resolved per request (no caching).

fields.name: isSystem
fields.label: System Role
fields.validation: true for the seeded SystemAdmin/User roles; system roles cannot be deleted
fields.purpose: Protects the seeded SystemAdmin and User roles from deletion so a tenant can never remove the only admin role and lock itself out; informational guard on the RBAC screen.

invariants: A13 audit + SSE on every committed change
effective permissions = deduplicated union of role + direct user grant + designation + business-unit, plus super-admin all; applied next request (no caching)
SystemAdmin (all) and User are seeded; system roles cannot be deleted
